Justification: The primary target users are individuals with mobility impairments, including but not limited to, spinal cord injuries, muscular dystrophy, cerebral palsy, multiple sclerosis, and age-related mobility decline.; Consideration will be given to users who require a wheelchair for daily living activities, including indoor and outdoor navigation, personal care, and social engagement.; The design will cater to a range of user anthropometrics, accommodating users from adolescence to elderly, with varying body weights and heights.; The PDS will also consider users with cognitive impairments who may require simplified controls and safety features.

The wheelchair must provide reliable and safe mobility on a variety of surfaces, including smooth indoor floors, uneven outdoor terrain (grass, gravel, mild inclines), and paved pathways.; Maximum speed will be electronically limited to a safe range (e.g., 6-10 km/h) for user safety and regulatory compliance.; The wheelchair must be capable of ascending inclines up to 10-15 degrees for moderate accessibility needs.; Minimum turning radius (e.g., < 1.5 meters) is critical for maneuverability in confined indoor spaces and crowded environments.; The wheelchair must be able to overcome small obstacles (e.g., curbs up to 5 cm) independently or with minimal user effort.; Sufficient ground clearance (e.g., > 10 cm) is required to navigate uneven terrain and avoid scraping on obstacles.

The electric wheelchair shall provide a minimum operational range of 25-30 kilometers on a single full charge to accommodate a full day's use for most users.; Battery technology will prioritize sustainability, with a preference for lithium-ion (LiFePO4) or similar advanced chemistries offering high energy density, long cycle life, and reduced environmental impact compared to lead-acid batteries.; The charging system will be efficient and user-friendly, with a charging time of no more than 6-8 hours for a full charge.; The battery management system (BMS) will include safety features such as overcharge protection, over-discharge protection, and temperature monitoring.; The battery pack will be designed for easy and safe removal for replacement or off-board charging, with secure locking mechanisms.

The seating system will be designed for optimal comfort and support during prolonged use, incorporating adjustable lumbar support, seat depth, and backrest angle.; Cushioning will utilize sustainable and breathable materials (e.g., recycled foam, natural fibers) to prevent pressure sores and enhance user comfort.; Armrests will be adjustable in height and width to provide comfortable support and facilitate transfers.; Footrests will be adjustable in height and angle to accommodate various leg lengths and postural needs, with non-slip surfaces.; The control interface (joystick) will be ergonomically designed for intuitive and precise control, with options for left or right-hand operation and adjustable sensitivity.; The overall design should minimize vibration and noise during operation for a more pleasant user experience.

The wheelchair will incorporate a robust braking system, including electromagnetic brakes that automatically engage when the joystick is released and a manual parking brake.; Anti-tip wheels will be standard to prevent the wheelchair from tipping backward on inclines or when encountering obstacles.; Seatbelts or harnesses will be provided for user security, with adjustable and easy-to-use fastening mechanisms.; Integrated lighting (front and rear LED lights) will enhance visibility in low-light conditions for increased safety.; Audible indicators (e.g., horn) will be included for alerting pedestrians and other users.; Emergency stop functionality will be easily accessible on the control panel.; The wheelchair will meet relevant international safety standards (e.g., ISO 7176 series, FDA regulations for medical devices).

The frame and structural components will be constructed from lightweight yet strong and durable materials, prioritizing recycled aluminum alloys or high-strength composites.; All components will be selected for their longevity and resistance to wear and tear, especially in frequently used parts like the joystick, motor, and wheels.; The wheelchair will be designed to withstand typical daily use, including minor impacts and vibrations, without compromising structural integrity.; Sealed bearings and robust motor enclosures will protect against dust and moisture ingress, enhancing reliability in various environmental conditions.; A modular design approach will facilitate easy maintenance, repair, and replacement of individual components, extending the product's lifespan and reducing waste.

Materials selection will prioritize recycled, recyclable, and renewable resources wherever feasible (e.g., recycled plastics for non-structural components, sustainably sourced wood for accents, recycled aluminum for frame).; The design will aim for energy efficiency in its operation, optimizing motor and battery management to minimize energy consumption.; The product lifecycle will be considered, with a focus on design for disassembly (DFD) to facilitate end-of-life recycling and material recovery.; Packaging will be minimized and made from recycled and recyclable materials.; The manufacturing process will aim to reduce waste, water usage, and energy consumption.; The product will be designed for longevity and repairability to reduce the frequency of replacement and associated environmental impact.

The wheelchair will feature a modern, sleek, and unobtrusive design that promotes user dignity and integration into social environments.; Color options will be offered to allow for personalization, with a focus on muted, natural tones or customizable accents.; The user interface will be intuitive and easy to understand, with clear labeling and visual feedback.; The overall user experience, from initial setup to daily operation and maintenance, will be designed to be positive and empowering.; The design will consider the psychological impact of using a mobility aid, aiming for a product that users are proud to use.

The wheelchair will be designed with ease of maintenance and repair in mind, utilizing standard tools and readily available replacement parts.; Key components will be easily accessible for inspection and servicing.; A comprehensive user manual and service guide will be provided, detailing maintenance procedures and troubleshooting steps.; Diagnostic capabilities will be integrated to assist technicians in identifying and resolving issues efficiently.; The modular design will allow for quick replacement of common wear-and-tear items, minimizing downtime for the user.

The target retail price will be set to be competitive within the accessible electric wheelchair market, aiming for a balance between advanced features and affordability.; Material and manufacturing choices will be optimized to control costs without compromising safety, durability, or sustainability.; The long-term cost of ownership will be considered, with an emphasis on low maintenance requirements and energy efficiency.; The design will explore options for modularity and tiered feature sets to cater to different budget levels.; Consideration will be given to potential for insurance reimbursement or governmental subsidies.

The product must comply with all relevant national and international regulations for medical devices and electric mobility aids.; This includes, but is not limited to, safety standards (e.g., ISO 7176 series), electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) regulations, and battery disposal regulations.; Certification processes (e.g., CE marking, FDA clearance) will be pursued as required for target markets.; Clear labeling and documentation will be provided to ensure compliance with all applicable laws and standards.

While not a primary requirement for the initial design, future iterations may incorporate optional connectivity features.; This could include Bluetooth or Wi-Fi for smartphone integration, allowing for remote diagnostics, battery monitoring, usage tracking, and potentially personalized control settings.; GPS tracking could be considered for enhanced user safety and recovery in case of emergencies.; The design will allow for potential future upgrades to incorporate these smart functionalities without requiring a complete redesign.
